briefdescription.para: Current finest level.
Stream a summary of the mesh hierarchy (level extents, cell counts, etc.).
Construct an empty mesh.

memberdef.argsstring: (const RealBox *rb, int max_level_in, const Vector< int > &n_cell_in, int coord=-1, Vector< IntVect > refrat=Vector< IntVect >(), const int *is_per=nullptr)
memberdef.name: AmrMesh

briefdescription.para: Construct using legacy inputs (same signature as AmrCore).

parameterdescription.para: Optional domain definition; nullptr uses defaults.
Coordinate system index (defaults to Cartesian if not specified in ParmParse parameter geometry.coord_sys).
Optional periodicity flags (defaults to non-periodic if not specified in ParmParse parameter geometry.is_periodic).

memberdef.argsstring: (const RealBox &rb, int max_level_in, const Vector< int > &n_cell_in, int coord, Vector< IntVect > const &a_refrat, Array< int, 3 > const &is_per)
memberdef.name: AmrMesh

briefdescription.para: Construct with explicit domain and periodicity array.

parameterdescription.para: Array of periodicity flags (0 = nonperiodic, 1 = periodic).

briefdescription.para: Construct from a pre-built Geometry and AmrInfo bundle.
Return the max level.
Return the finest level.
Return the refinement ratio for level lev.
Return the maximum refinement ratio in any direction.
Return refinement ratios between all levels.
Override the max grid size constraint for all levels (scalar version).
Override the max grid size constraint using explicit IntVect new_mgs.
Override per-level max grid sizes using scalar-per-level vector new_mgs.
Override per-level max grid sizes using explicit IntVect-per-level vector new_mgs.
Override the blocking factor constraint for all levels (scalar version).

parameterdescription.para: Blocking factor to enforce in every direction.

briefdescription.para: Override blocking factor using explicit IntVect new_bf.
Override per-level blocking factors using scalar-per-level vector new_bf.
Override per-level blocking factors using explicit IntVect vector new_bf.
Set the minimum acceptable grid efficiency to eff.
Set the number of cell blocks required for proper nesting to n.
Update the recorded finest level index to new_finest_level (without reallocating).
Install a DistributionMapping for level lev.
Install a BoxArray for level lev.
Install a Geometry object for level lev.
Given domain box, return AMR level. Return -1 if there is no match.
Clear the stored DistributionMapping for level lev.
Clear the stored BoxArray for level lev.
Return the number of buffer cells (as a single integer) in error estimator.
Return the number of buffer cells (as an IntVect) in error estimator.
Return the minimum allowable grid efficiency.
Return the number of cell blocks to define proper nesting.
Return the blocking factor at level lev.
Return the largest allowable grid.
Return true if valid grids/dmaps exist for level lev.
Should we keep the coarser grids fixed (and not regrid those levels) at all?
Up to what level should we keep the coarser grids fixed (and not regrid those levels)?
Try to chop grids on level lev until BoxArray ba has at least target_size boxes.

parameterdescription.para: Level whose blocking and efficiency rules apply.

briefdescription.para: Make a level-0 BoxArray covering the whole domain (without installing it).

memberdef.argsstring: (int lbase, Real time, int &new_finest, Vector< BoxArray > &new_grids)
memberdef.name: MakeNewGrids

briefdescription.para: Make new grids based on error estimates. This function expects that valid BoxArrays exist in this->grids from level lbase to level this->finest_level (the current finest level). new_grids. On return, the new finest level is stored in new_finest, and the new grids are stored in new_grids from Array element lbase+1 to new_finest_level (unless fixed grids are used). Note that this function adds at most one more level to the existing levels, and it may remove all levels above the base level. This function does not change the value of this->finest_level, nor does it modifies any BoxArrays stored in this->grids. It also does not modify new_grids's elements outside the range [lbase+1,new_finest_level].

detaileddescription.para: Build new grids above level lbase using current error tags.

parameterdescription.para: Output: new BoxArrays for levels > lbase (vector sized finest+1).

briefdescription.para: Convenience overload that rebuilds every level (including level 0) at time time.

memberdef.argsstring: (int lev, Real time, const BoxArray &ba, const DistributionMapping &dm)
memberdef.name: MakeNewLevelFromScratch

briefdescription.para: Hook invoked by MakeNewGrids() to build a fresh level.

memberdef.argsstring: (int lev, TagBoxArray &tags, Real time, int ngrow)
memberdef.name: ErrorEst

briefdescription.para: Hook for tagging cells on level lev.

parameterdescription.para: Number of ghost layers filled in tags.

briefdescription.para: Manual tagging hook. Tags are defined on level lev grids coarsened by bf_lev[lev].
Allow derived classes to edit the base-level grids before installing them.

detaileddescription.para: For example, use this function if you want to remove covered grids on the coarsest refinement level.
This is called by MakeNewGrids() after computing the coarsest-level BoxArray and before invoking MakeNewLevelFromScratch().

briefdescription.para: Return a BoxArray that should never be tagged on level lev (e.g., buffer zones).
Count the total number of cells at level lev.
Build a distribution map for level lev grid layout ba (override to customize load balancing).
Validate AmrInfo inputs (throws if inconsistent).
Disable the iterate-on-new-grids loop for the next MakeNewGrids call.
Force MakeNewGrids to use the newer chopping algorithm.

memberdef.argsstring: (int max_level_in, const Vector< int > &n_cell_in, Vector< IntVect > refrat=Vector< IntVect >(), const RealBox *rb=nullptr, int coord=-1, const int *is_per=nullptr)
memberdef.name: InitAmrMesh
